title: Generic global CSS selectors can collide outside the component

evidence.quote:
<style>{`
        @keyframes drift1 {
...
        .orb1 { animation: drift1 40s ease-in-out infinite; will-change: transform; }
        .orb2 { animation: drift2 35s ease-in-out infinite; will-change: transform; }
        .orb3 { animation: drift3 30s ease-in-out infinite; will-change: transform; }

reasoning: The component injects global class selectors and keyframe names with very generic names (`orb1`, `drift1`, etc.). Any unrelated element or stylesheet using the same names can unexpectedly inherit these animations or override this component's animation definitions, making behavior depend on mount/order of global CSS.
recommendation: Use scoped CSS module classes, more specific component-prefixed names, or inline `animationName` values generated for this component to avoid global namespace collisions.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests exercise coexistence with other stylesheets or multiple components that might define matching class/keyframe names.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a render/style test with another `.orb1` rule or component-specific class names to verify the animated background does not affect unrelated elements.
